LAW AND MOTION 302, JUDGE BUSCH DISCLOSED THAT HIS WIFE CATHERINE BARNES, A DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, IS NOT INVOLVED IN THIS CASE AND DOES NOT HANDLE LITIGATION. JUDGE BUSCH DOES NOT VIEW HER EMPLOYMENT AS A BASIS FOR HIS RECUSAL. NO OBJECTIONS WERE RAISED. THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF EDDIE VALADEZ' MOTION FOR SANCTIONS PURSUANT TO CCP 128.7 IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO BE HEARD ON MAY 28, 2013 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF EDDIE VALADEZ' MOTION TO SUPPLEMENTAL PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF IS ATTEMPTING TO PLEAD NEW THEORIES OF LIABILITY THAT ARE UNRELATED TO THE CHAIN OF EVENTS THAT WERE ORIGINALLY PLEADED. A PARTY CANNOT ALLEGE AN ENTIRELY NEW CAUSE OF ACTION IN A SUPPLEMENTAL COMPLAINT. SEE FLOOD V. SIMPSON, (1975) 45 CAL.APP.3D 644, 647. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

Notice Of Motion To Supplemental Pltf'S Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF EDDIE VALADEZ' Motion To Supplemental Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint is denied. Plaintiff is attempting to plead new theories of liability that are unrelated to the chain of events that were originally pleaded. A party cannot allege an entirely new cause of action in a supplemental complaint. See Flood v. Simpson, (1975) 45 Cal.App.3d 644, 647. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Oct-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Kristen Ross, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak, Tel. 415.835.6777, for plaintiff and opposing party.
No other appearance.

RULING: The court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS as to defendants Larry Nichol and Edward Matias (misnamed Larry Nichols and Edward Mathias). The court determines that plaintiff served the person apparently in charge within the meaning of CCP 415.20 because defendants received actual notice of the action and the Court liberally construes the service statutes where the defendants receive actual notice. Dill v. Berquist Construction Co. (1994) 24 Cal.App.4th 1426, 1436-1437. The misspelling of the defendants' names does not render the service fatally defective. Plaintiff is instructed to file an amended complaint with the correct spellings. The Court denies the motion as moot as to defendant Mark Calgaro because evidence indicates that he was personally served on September 28, 2012. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.
